research_questions.title: Evidence-Based ESG Certification
research_questions.question: How can ESG certification be modeled as a multi-level, evidence-grounded framework rather than a purely self-reported questionnaire?

research_questions.title: Verification & Reliability
research_questions.question: To what extent does document-level and media-based verification improve the reliability of ESG self-assessments?

research_questions.title: Explainability & Transparency
research_questions.question: How can explainable mechanisms be integrated into ESG certification to support trust, auditability, and regulatory compliance?

research_questions.title: Graph-Based Reasoning
research_questions.question: How can graph-based representations enhance ESG reasoning, traceability, and cross-company analysis?

research_questions.title: Industry Sensitivity
research_questions.question: How does industry-aware clustering affect ESG performance interpretation and benchmarking?

objectives.text: Design a two-level ESG certification framework (self-assessment and evidence-validated)
Integrate document and media verification into ESG assessment workflows
Develop explainable ESG scoring and reporting mechanisms
Model ESG certification using graph-based representations
Enable industry-aware ESG benchmarking and clustering

methodology.label: Self-Assessment Design
methodology.description: Design structured ESG self-assessment questions with single, multiple, and essay-based responses.

methodology.label: Document Verification
methodology.description: Collect supporting documents and apply OCR to extract verifiable ESG evidence.

methodology.label: Media Screening
methodology.description: Analyze news and social media signals over a 1–2 year window to identify ESG-related risks.

methodology.label: Scoring & Normalization
methodology.description: Aggregate self-assessment and evidence signals into normalized ESG scores.

methodology.label: Explainability Layer
methodology.description: Generate traceable explanations linking scores to evidence, risks, and gaps.

methodology.label: Graph Construction
methodology.description: Construct a knowledge graph linking companies, questions, documents, media, and risks.

methodology.label: Industry Clustering
methodology.description: Cluster companies by industry to enable sector-adjusted ESG benchmarking.

hypotheses.text: Evidence-supported ESG assessments exhibit lower score variance than self-reported assessments alone
Explainable ESG reports increase perceived trust and usability among stakeholders
Graph-based ESG representations improve the detection of inconsistencies and risk signals
Industry-aware ESG clustering significantly alters ESG performance interpretation
